text: Chiral nanographenes represent a newly explored class of Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) that combine the appealing inherent chirality phenomenon with the exciting electronic, optical and magnetic properties of graphene analogues. Nonplanar chiral forms such as, but not limited to, contorted acenes and helicene–graphene hybrids have attracted large interest in terms of potential uses in future nanoelectronics and nanooptronics. Progress on control of their chiroptical behavior including CD and CPL responses has been achieved. This article discusses recent advances in the syntheses of chiral nanographenes emphasizing strategies other than traditional Scholl reactions. New architectures such as expanded and π-expanded helicenes, imide-based nanographenes and multiple helices as well as nonalternant PAHs comprising non-hexagonal rings have emerged. The present review covers as much as possible the synthesis, conformational stability, and associated unusual physical properties associated with such novel chiral systems. It is demonstrated here that by tuning the extent of contortion together with different aspects of materials science related to spintronics and electronics have been opened.
